dailyJudgementSnippet: OpenAI won a weak day mostly because Codex drew the only clear product praise in the relevant discussions: commenters called it more generous on usage and more accurate for backend/debugging work, even while noting some UI and personality rough edges; that modest upside beat a news cycle otherwise dominated by quota, cache, and trust complaints about rivals [E3][E1][E2][E5].
winnerExplanation: OpenAI finished on top because HN commenters gave Codex the strongest concrete endorsement of any tracked provider, saying it was more accurate and less frustrating on usage than competing tools in a directly relevant comparison thread [E3]. OpenAI still took a smaller negative hit from complaints that ChatGPT felt watered down [E4], but Anthropic faced two separate front-page threads centered on Claude Code quota drain, hidden cache changes, and declining usefulness [E1][E2], while Google Gemini only showed up through a quota backlash comparison [E5].

openai.judgementSnippet: Codex got the day's clearest endorsement when commenters said it was more generous with usage and better at backend logic and debugging than Claude for their work, though they still complained about its tone and weaker UI polish; a separate comment about ChatGPT being "watered down" kept the overall read only mildly positive rather than strong [E3][E4].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Two separate front-page threads hit Claude Code on the same themes: fast quota burn, cache behavior that made stale sessions expensive, frustration with hidden or poorly explained changes, and a broader feeling that the paid product had become less reliable and less recommendable over the past month [E1][E2].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Gemini was not the main story, but it was pulled into the discussion as another example of quota disappointment: a commenter described a bait-and-switch around Pro and Ultra expectations and said they had moved key workflows off Gemini tools despite still paying for the subscription [E5].

evidence.summary: The Claude Code quota-exhaustion story produced extensive complaints about rapid quota drain, long exploration loops, expensive stale-session cache misses, and users no longer recommending the paid product; an Anthropic team reply explained mitigations but did not change the net negative tone.

A second front-page thread accused Anthropic of downgrading Claude Code cache TTL, with commenters describing worse UX, faster quota burn, poorer model performance, and spreading distrust because changes felt hidden.

Inside the Claude Code quota discussion, commenters said OpenAI's Codex plan felt more generous on usage and more accurate for backend logic, hard debugging, and complex problem-solving, making OpenAI the only provider to get notable direct product praise.
The Anthropic cache-TTL thread also contained a direct complaint that ChatGPT was being watered down, adding a weaker but relevant negative signal for OpenAI.

A commenter used the Claude Code quota thread to cite recent backlash around Gemini CLI and Google Pro/Ultra quotas, calling it a bait-and-switch and saying they had moved IDE and CLI workflows away from Gemini.
